instruction_summary: Continue handling open issues that do not require additional requirement decisions, keep .tmp.requirements-checklist.md in sync with the current open-issue state, and move forward on open issues whose initial scope is now concrete enough to implement.
actual_work: Used .tmp.requirements-checklist.md as the hold list, confirmed GitHub CLI was unavailable in this environment, and initially inferred concrete next slices from recent merged PRs. Completed the remaining issue #152 wiring for UTF-8 download filenames by applying existing Content-Disposition helpers to direct document-file downloads and ZIP download responses, advanced issue #118 by adding an admin project-template preview/apply workflow on the project edit screen, advanced issue #127 by adding project-scoped document catalog index/show routes with viewer-aware item filtering, advanced issue #119 by adding an admin document usage report screen backed by the existing usage-report services, and advanced issue #117 by adding user/admin access-request screens plus approval/cancellation flows around visible document/file download requests. Then advanced issue #109 by exposing internal review comments on DocumentVersion detail for internal users, allowing comment creation, and allowing admin resolve/reject actions while keeping external users blocked. After the user pointed out stale checklist state, re-fetched open issues from GitHub via the connector, confirmed issue #154 was already closed, and updated .tmp.requirements-checklist.md to remove stale blocked entries whose issue bodies now define an initial implementation scope. Continued with issue #112 by wiring an admin external-preview screen for projects, reusing the existing ExternalVisibilityPreview services to preview document/file visibility for a selected external user or all active external viewers in a selected company, adding an edit-screen entry point, recording preview runs in AccessLog with target_type external_preview, and then closing issue #112 once the MVP acceptance criteria were covered. Then advanced issue #113 by exposing the already-implemented DocumentVersion quality-check services through a new version-level screen plus JSON/Markdown endpoints for internal viewers only, linked that screen from DocumentVersion detail, and later extended the checker itself to inspect attached Markdown sources for broken relative document links, image references, and attachment references while treating missing rendered-site entry files as build-failure-equivalent errors before closing issue #113. Then advanced issue #115 by wiring the existing AI context export services into a project-level HTML/JSON/Markdown export flow, linking it from project detail, keeping export contents scoped to the current viewer's visible documents, logging AI context preview/export accesses in AccessLog with target_type ai_context, and closing issue #115 after the project-level export flow and representative request coverage were in place. Then advanced a concrete slice of issue #111 by enforcing existing publication-window fields consistently for external viewers across project/detail/document listing, direct document detail, attachment download, AI context export, and the shared document tree helper. Then advanced a concrete slice of issue #123 by wiring ZIP export options for path mode, markdown-source inclusion, attachment inclusion, and PDF-only output into the project/version ZIP builders and the document index bulk ZIP form. Then documented the viewer-adapter direction and service-boundary guidance in repo docs, split the minimal document approval-request flow into new issue #264, and closed planning/design issues #137, #260, and #261. Then documented the data-classification direction in app spec, added a dedicated OSS text-viewer comparison memo under docs/, linked it from docs/README.md, and closed design/research issues #160 and #263. Then moved three remaining umbrella/defer decisions into docs/ToDo.md and closed placeholder issues #259 (automatic retries defer), #138 (broad UI/UX umbrella), and #136 (multilingual management defer). Then documented that broad quality/ops stabilization work should no longer stay open as umbrella issues, added that guidance to docs/ToDo.md, and closed broad placeholder issues #139, #140, #143, #144, #145, and #146 in favor of reopening only concrete failing tests, dependency fixes, migration/constraint changes, performance regressions, or observability tasks. Then documented that long-running job umbrellas and regeneration placeholders should only be reopened when concrete synchronous bottlenecks or operational demand exist, and closed umbrella/placeholder issues #153, #157, and #258. Then documented that vendor-specific gem survey issues should not stay open and that import/build jobization should only be revisited when operator-facing synchronous execution becomes a real bottleneck, then closed placeholder issues #98 and #257. Then closed issue #124 based on the existing rule-based classification implementation (`DocumentClassificationSuggester`, YAML rule config, frontmatter precedence, matched_rules in import dry-run output) and advanced issue #131 by surfacing document importance metadata in project/detail/list views, prioritizing project and document index ordering with `recommended_first`, adding a project-level important-documents section, and adding request coverage before closing the issue. Then advanced issue #125 by integrating duplicate candidates into import dry-run validation and both hash/markdown presenters, narrowing update-candidate detection to exact source-path or unique same-file-name matches within a project and surfacing similar-document reasons without auto-merging before closing the issue. Then re-fetched the current open-issue list with `gh`, compared it against existing models/controllers/specs, determined that issue #132 already had an implemented MVP dashboard (`/dashboard`) covering accessible projects, favorites, read-later items, recent documents, and recently updated readable documents, updated the app spec to document that contract, and closed the issue. Then advanced issue #262 by adding viewer-scoped typography improvements to the application layout, switching inline text attachments to a Rails-side line-numbered preview page, injecting portal reading CSS into rendered Docusaurus HTML while hiding extra site chrome, documenting the new viewer contract in the app spec, and closing the issue. Then advanced issue #114 by wiring admin CRUD for `DocumentSet`, adding project-scoped list/detail screens, allowing ordered item selection with optional fixed `DocumentVersion`, filtering visible items for external viewers, and documenting the initial `DocumentSet` contract in the app spec. Then closed issue #111 by adopting the simplified initial publication-window approach explicitly: `DocumentVersion.published_from/published_until` are the source of truth, external viewers are filtered dynamically in project list/detail and related export/download flows, projects with no currently visible documents are hidden from external listings, and internal/admin users can inspect the current publication-window state on version detail. Then expanded issue #109 from version-only internal comments to a fuller internal review MVP: `DocumentReviewComment` now supports document-level comments, optional text line/anchor/source metadata for text-based documents, and parent comment references for future thread/Q&A expansion; document detail and version detail both expose the internal review form; and the updated contract is documented in the app spec. Then implemented and closed issue #264 by adding `DocumentApprovalRequest`, Document detail creation flow, internal-only list/detail screens, and OK / Cancel actions with `acted_by` and timestamp recording, while keeping requester access to their own detail page. Finally, closed issue #155 by strengthening safeguards around the currently implemented dangerous operations: admin delete buttons now carry resource-specific confirmation text with impact hints, and standard template application now requires a confirmation step that includes create/skip counts from the existing preview. Added or extended request/spec coverage for code slices and updated repo docs to state the new contracts.

remaining_concerns: Issue #109 is only partially advanced here; the broader DocumentComment/common-thread design described in the issue body is not yet implemented.

Issue #111 is only partially advanced here; the initial visibility boundary now respects existing publication-window fields for external viewers, but the issue body's separate DocumentPublication model, admin editing flow, and project-level publication management are not implemented yet.
Issue #123 is only partially advanced here; ZIP path/file-type controls are wired, but actual PDF watermark rendering and classification-tag-driven output behavior are not implemented yet.

signals.note: Created the repo-local prompt log before deeper issue triage.
Checked GitHub CLI availability early and fell back once auth/connectivity issues were confirmed.
